title: Lighting the Lamp of Kannada 🪔✨
poemText:
We shall light the lamp of Kannada
The lamp of Karunadu, the lamp of rich speech
The winning lamp held with love
We shall light the lamp of Kannada
bhavartha: This poem celebrates the Kannada language and culture, symbolizing it as a lamp that illuminates the land.

title: Daffodils 🌼🍃
poemText:
I wandered lonely as a cloud
That floats on high o'er vales and hills,
When all at once I saw a crowd,
A host, of golden daffodils;
Beside the lake, beneath the trees,
Fluttering and dancing in the breeze.
bhavartha: The poet describes the joy and beauty of seeing a field of daffodils by a lake.

title: The Road Not Taken 🛣️🌲
poemText:
Two roads diverged in a yellow wood,
And sorry I could not travel both
And be one traveler, long I stood
And looked down one as far as I could
To where it bent in the undergrowth;
bhavartha: The poem explores the choices we make in life and how they shape our future.

wordMeanings.Undergrowth: Small trees and plants growing beneath larger trees

title: O Captain! My Captain! 🚢⚓
poemText:
O Captain! my Captain! our fearful trip is done,
The ship has weather'd every rack, the prize we sought is won,
The port is near, the bells I hear, the people all exulting,
While follow eyes the steady keel, the vessel grim and daring;
bhavartha: An elegy written on the death of Abraham Lincoln, celebrating his leadership during the Civil War.

wordMeanings.Keel: The long piece of wood or steel along the bottom of a ship

title: Still I Rise 🌅✊
poemText:
You may write me down in history
With your bitter, twisted lies,
You may trod me in the very dirt
But still, like dust, I'll rise.
bhavartha: A powerful poem about resilience and overcoming oppression.

title: The Raven 🦅🌙
poemText:
Once upon a midnight dreary, while I pondered, weak and weary,
Over many a quaint and curious volume of forgotten lore—
While I nodded, nearly napping, suddenly there came a tapping,
As of some one gently rapping, rapping at my chamber door.
bhavartha: A narrative poem about mysterious loss and mourning.

title: If— 🦁👑
poemText:
If you can keep your head when all about you
Are losing theirs and blaming it on you,
If you can trust yourself when all men doubt you,
But make allowance for their doubting too;
bhavartha: A poem about stoicism, self-control, and moral integrity.

title: Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening 🌲❄️
poemText:
Whose woods these are I think I know.
His house is in the village though;
He will not see me stopping here
To watch his woods fill up with snow.
bhavartha: A contemplative poem about nature's beauty and life's obligations.

title: Sonnet 18 🌹📜
poemText:
Shall I compare thee to a summer's day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate:
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of May,
And summer's lease hath all too short a date.
bhavartha: A sonnet praising the beloved's eternal beauty through poetry.

title: The Tyger 🐯🔥
poemText:
Tyger Tyger, burning bright,
In the forests of the night;
What immortal hand or eye,
Could frame thy fearful symmetry?
bhavartha: Questions the creation of evil and the nature of God.

title: Annabel Lee 🌊👻
poemText:
It was many and many a year ago,
In a kingdom by the sea,
That a maiden there lived whom you may know
By the name of Annabel Lee;
bhavartha: A tragic love poem about eternal devotion beyond death.

title: Do Not Go Gentle into That Good Night ⭐🌙
bhavartha: A plea to fight against death and embrace life passionately.

title: I Wandered Lonely as a Cloud ☁️🌼
poemText:
I wandered lonely as a cloud
That floats on high o'er vales and hills,
When all at once I saw a crowd,
A host, of golden daffodils;
bhavartha: Celebrates the beauty of nature and its lasting impact on memory.

title: The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock 🎭🌃
bhavartha: Explores modern alienation and the paralysis of indecision.

title: Ozymandias 🏛️🏜️
poemText:
I met a traveller from an antique land,
Who said—'Two vast and trunkless legs of stone
Stand in the desert. . . . Near them, on the sand,
Half sunk a shattered visage lies'
bhavartha: Reflects on the impermanence of power and human achievement.

title: Kubla Khan 🏰🌊
poemText:
In Xanadu did Kubla Khan
A stately pleasure-dome decree:
Where Alph, the sacred river, ran
Through caverns measureless to man
bhavartha: A dream-like vision of an exotic paradise.

title: The Solitary Reaper 🌾🎵
poemText:
Behold her, single in the field,
Yon solitary Highland Lass!
Reaping and singing by herself;
Stop here, or gently pass!
bhavartha: Celebrates the beauty of a solitary worker's song in nature.

title: La Belle Dame sans Merci 🌹⚔️
poemText:
O what can ail thee, knight-at-arms,
Alone and palely loitering?
The sedge has withered from the lake,
And no birds sing.
bhavartha: A ballad about a knight enchanted by a beautiful fairy woman.

title: Ulysses ⚓🌊
poemText:
It little profits that an idle king,
By this still hearth, among these barren crags,
Match'd with an aged wife, I mete and dole
Unequal laws unto a savage race
bhavartha: A dramatic monologue about the desire for adventure and purpose.

title: The Lake Isle of Innisfree 🏝️🍃
poemText:
I will arise and go now, and go to Innisfree,
And a small cabin build there, of clay and wattles made:
Nine bean-rows will I have there, a hive for the honey-bee,
And live alone in the bee-loud glade.
bhavartha: Expresses longing for a peaceful, simple life in nature.

title: The Red Wheelbarrow 🚜🌿
poemText:
so much depends
upon

a red wheel
barrow

glazed with rain
water

beside the white
chickens.
bhavartha: Celebrates the beauty in ordinary, everyday objects.

title: Because I could not stop for Death 💀🚗
poemText:
Because I could not stop for Death –
He kindly stopped for me –
The Carriage held but just Ourselves –
And Immortality.
bhavartha: Personifies death as a gentleman caller taking the speaker on a journey.

title: Hope is the thing with feathers 🕊️✨
poemText:
Hope is the thing with feathers
That perches in the soul,
And sings the tune without the words,
And never stops at all
bhavartha: Uses metaphor to describe hope as a bird that never abandons the soul.

title: Wild Geese 🦆🌾
poemText:
You do not have to be good.
You do not have to walk on your knees
for a hundred miles through the desert repenting.
You only have to let the soft animal of your body
love what it loves.
bhavartha: Encourages self-acceptance and connection with nature.

title: The New Colossus 🗽🌊
poemText:
Not like the brazen giant of Greek fame,
With conquering limbs astride from land to land;
Here at our sea-washed, sunset gates shall stand
A mighty woman with a torch
bhavartha: The poem inscribed on the Statue of Liberty, welcoming immigrants.

title: Invictus 🦅💪
poemText:
Out of the night that covers me,
Black as the pit from pole to pole,
I thank whatever gods may be
For my unconquerable soul.
bhavartha: A poem of resilience and self-determination in the face of adversity.

title: The Waste Land 🏚️🌫️
poemText:
April is the cruellest month, breeding
Lilacs out of the dead land, mixing
Memory and desire, stirring
Dull roots with spring rain.
bhavartha: A landmark modernist poem depicting post-war disillusionment.

title: How Do I Love Thee? 💕📜
poemText:
How do I love thee? Let me count the ways.
I love thee to the depth and breadth and height
My soul can reach, when feeling out of sight
For the ends of being and ideal grace.
bhavartha: A sonnet expressing the many dimensions of love.

title: She Walks in Beauty ✨🌙
poemText:
She walks in beauty, like the night
Of cloudless climes and starry skies;
And all that's best of dark and bright
Meet in her aspect and her eyes.
bhavartha: Celebrates the beauty of a woman through natural imagery.

title: Ode to a Nightingale 🐦🌙
poemText:
My heart aches, and a drowsy numbness pains
My sense, as though of hemlock I had drunk,
Or emptied some dull opiate to the drains
One minute past, and Lethe-wards had sunk
bhavartha: Explores themes of mortality, escape, and the power of art.

title: Ode to the West Wind 🌬️🍂
poemText:
O wild West Wind, thou breath of Autumn's being,
Thou, from whose unseen presence the leaves dead
Are driven, like ghosts from an enchanter fleeing
bhavartha: Addresses the wind as a powerful force of change and inspiration.

title: The Lady of Shalott 🏰🚣‍♀️
poemText:
On either side of the river lie
Long fields of barley and of rye,
That clothe the wold and meet the sky;
And through the field the road runs by
bhavartha: A ballad about a cursed woman isolated in a tower.

title: Paul Revere's Ride 🐎🔔
poemText:
Listen, my children, and you shall hear
Of the midnight ride of Paul Revere,
On the eighteenth of April, in Seventy-Five;
Hardly a man is now alive
bhavartha: Narrates Paul Revere's midnight ride to warn of British troops.

title: The Song of Hiawatha 🏹🌲
poemText:
By the shores of Gitche Gumee,
By the shining Big-Sea-Water,
Stood the wigwam of Nokomis,
Daughter of the Moon, Nokomis.
bhavartha: An epic poem based on Native American legends.

title: The Pied Piper of Hamelin 🐀🎵
poemText:
Hamelin Town's in Brunswick,
By famous Hanover city;
The river Weser, deep and wide,
Washes its wall on the riverside.
bhavartha: A narrative poem about a mysterious piper who rid a town of rats.

title: Gunga Din 🐘💧
poemText:
You may talk o' gin and beer
When you're quartered safe out 'ere,
An' you're sent to penny-fights an' Aldershot it;
But when it comes to slaughter
bhavartha: Honors an Indian water carrier who saved a British soldier.

title: The Highwayman 🐎🌙
poemText:
The wind was a torrent of darkness among the gusty trees,
The moon was a ghostly galleon tossed upon cloudy seas,
The road was a ribbon of moonlight over the purple moor,
bhavartha: A tragic ballad of love and betrayal.

title: The Listeners 🚪🌙
poemText:
'Is there anybody there?' said the Traveller,
Knocking on the moonlit door;
And his horse in the silence champed the grasses
Of the forest's ferny floor.
bhavartha: A mysterious poem about a traveller knocking at a deserted house.

title: Sea-Fever ⛵🌊
poemText:
I must go down to the seas again, to the lonely sea and the sky,
And all I ask is a tall ship and a star to steer her by,
And the wheel's kick and the wind's song and the white sail's shaking,
bhavartha: Expresses the poet's longing to return to the sea.

title: Adonais 🌹👼
poemText:
I weep for Adonais—he is dead!
O, weep for Adonais! though our tears
Thaw not the frost which binds so dear a head!
bhavartha: An elegy for the poet John Keats.

title: In Memoriam A.H.H. 💔🌹
poemText:
Strong Son of God, immortal Love,
Whom we, that have not seen thy face,
By faith, and faith alone, embrace,
Believing where we cannot prove;
bhavartha: A long elegy mourning the death of Tennyson's friend Arthur Hallam.

title: Dover Beach 🌊🏖️
poemText:
The sea is calm tonight,
The tide is full, the moon lies fair
Upon the straits; on the French coast the light
Gleams and is gone;
bhavartha: Reflects on the loss of religious faith in the modern world.

title: God's Grandeur ☀️🌾
poemText:
The world is charged with the grandeur of God.
It will flame out, like shining from shook foil;
It gathers to a greatness, like the ooze of oil
Crushed.
bhavartha: Celebrates the presence of God in the natural world.

title: The Darkling Thrush 🐦🌲
poemText:
I leant upon a coppice gate
When Frost was spectre-grey,
And Winter's dregs made desolate
The weakening eye of day.
bhavartha: A poem finding hope in the midst of winter's darkness.

title: The Second Coming ☠️🌀
bhavartha: A prophetic poem about chaos and the approach of a new era.

title: The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock (Excerpt) 🎭🌃
poemText:
In the room the women come and go
Talking of Michelangelo.
The yellow fog that rubs its back upon the window-panes
bhavartha: Explores modern anxiety and the paralysis of action.

title: Birches 🌳❄️
poemText:
When I see birches bend to left and right
Across the lines of straighter darker trees,
I like to think some boy's been swinging them.
bhavartha: Reflects on childhood, memory, and the balance between reality and imagination.

title: Mending Wall 🧱🌿
poemText:
Something there is that doesn't love a wall,
That sends the frozen-ground-swell under it,
And spills the upper boulders in the sun;
And makes gaps even two can pass abreast.
bhavartha: Questions the necessity and value of boundaries between people.

title: After Apple-Picking 🍎🍂
poemText:
My long two-pointed ladder's sticking through a tree
Toward heaven still,
And there's a barrel that I didn't fill
Beside it, and there may be two or three
bhavartha: Reflects on labor, exhaustion, and the passage of time.

title: Design 🕷️🌸
poemText:
I found a dimpled spider, fat and white,
On a white heal-all, holding up a moth
Like a white piece of rigid satin cloth—
Assorted characters of death and blight
bhavartha: Questions whether there is design or purpose in nature's cruelty.
